overall.description: A first-person view from the bridge of a vessel navigating through stormy seas at night, featuring operational navigation displays including radar and chart plotters. The scene includes a brief lightning strike illuminating the rough ocean waters, suggesting a maritime simulator environment.

scene_state.functional_zone: ship navigation bridge with electronic displays

events.description: lightning strike flashes on the port side, briefly illuminating the rough ocean surface

agents.ego_description: navigating the ship through stormy seas while monitoring radar and navigation displays

entered.detail: LCD display showing coastal map with vessel position and shoreline topography
circular radar screen with rotating sweep arm showing surrounding contacts and range rings
blue-screen display showing vessel telemetry and status information
metal deck with safety railings extending forward from the bridge windows
rough stormy sea with visible wave action and whitecaps

segments.scene_description: View from inside a ship's navigation bridge showing three operational displays while the vessel moves through rough stormy waters at night. The radar sweep rotates continuously, and a lightning bolt briefly illuminates the ocean surface to port, highlighting the severe weather conditions outside the sheltered bridge.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot captures a continuous view from within a ship's navigation bridge as it contends with a violent nocturnal storm. It begins focused on three operational displays, where the rhythmic rotation of the radar sweep tracks the vessel's progress through the darkness. As the ship pitches through rough waters, the sheltered interior of the bridge contrasts with the chaos visible through the windows. The scene then shifts in intensity when a sudden lightning bolt flashes across the horizon, momentarily illuminating the churning ocean surface to port. This burst of light reveals the severity of the weather before the bridge returns to its dim, instrument-lit atmosphere.
